ben-nevis-ben-nevis.photography_notes: Scotland's highest peak offers dramatic cloud inversions and north face views. The summit plateau can be featureless in mist but extraordinary above the clouds.

key_viewpoints.name: Summit Plateau Edge
key_viewpoints.description: The cliff edge above the north face provides vertigo-inducing views down 700m cliffs. Spectacular cloud inversions form below.

key_viewpoints.name: CMD Arete
key_viewpoints.description: The knife-edge ridge connecting to Carn Mor Dearg provides the classic profile view of Ben Nevis north face.

torridon-beinn-eighe.photography_notes: The quartzite cap of Beinn Eighe creates unique white-capped peaks. Triple Buttress is one of Scotland's finest mountain features.

key_viewpoints.name: Loch Clair
key_viewpoints.description: Classic reflection shot of Beinn Eighe and Liathach mirrored in the loch. Best in calm morning conditions.

key_viewpoints.name: Triple Buttress Viewpoint
key_viewpoints.description: From the approach path, the Triple Buttress is illuminated by afternoon light.

torridon-liathach.photography_notes: The most imposing mountain in Torridon with dramatic pinnacles and terraced sandstone. The traverse offers extraordinary ridge photography.

key_viewpoints.name: Glen Torridon Road
key_viewpoints.description: The A896 provides multiple pull-offs for capturing the full 8km ridge of Liathach in side-light.

glencoe-buachaille-etive-mor.photography_notes: One of Scotland's most photographed mountains. The pyramidal shape from the A82 is iconic. Dawn light catches the east face spectacularly.

key_viewpoints.name: A82 Viewpoint (Rannoch Moor)
key_viewpoints.description: The classic viewpoint on the A82 looking south-east to the perfect pyramidal form. River and foreground pools add depth.

key_viewpoints.name: Lagangarbh Hut
key_viewpoints.description: The cottage below Buachaille provides classic Scottish bothy-and-mountain composition.

glencoe-bidean-nam-bian.photography_notes: The Three Sisters of Glencoe form one of Scotland's most dramatic valley views. Best seen from the A82 or from the Lost Valley.

key_viewpoints.name: Three Sisters Viewpoint (A82)
key_viewpoints.description: Pull-off on the A82 gives the classic view of all three ridges descending into the glen. Dramatic in stormy light.

skye-cuillin-sgurr-nan-gillean.photography_notes: The most recognisable Cuillin peak. Sligachan Bridge provides the classic composition with river and mountain.

key_viewpoints.name: Sligachan Old Bridge
key_viewpoints.description: The iconic old bridge with Sgurr nan Gillean behind. Use long exposure on the river for silky water. Classic Scotland shot.

skye-cuillin-bla-bheinn.photography_notes: Bla Bheinn is the most accessible Cuillin peak for photography, with dramatic views across Loch Slapin and to the main ridge.

key_viewpoints.name: Torrin Viewpoint
key_viewpoints.description: Classic view across Loch Slapin with Bla Bheinn reflected. Works best at high tide with calm water.

cairngorms-ben-macdui.photography_notes: Scotland's second highest peak. The Cairngorms plateau offers vast tundra-like landscapes and arctic conditions in winter.

key_viewpoints.name: Coire an t-Sneachda
key_viewpoints.description: The northern corries provide dramatic cliff and snow features. Winter conditions create Arctic-like scenes.

coigach-suilven.photography_notes: Suilven's extraordinary sugar-loaf profile makes it one of Scotland's most distinctive mountains. The surrounding lochan-studded wilderness is itself photogenic.

key_viewpoints.name: Elphin Road Viewpoint
key_viewpoints.description: From the road near Elphin, Suilven's full profile rises above the Assynt landscape with foreground lochs.

coigach-stac-pollaidh.photography_notes: The sandstone pinnacles on the summit ridge are extraordinary in low light. Quick access makes dawn/dusk sessions feasible.

key_viewpoints.name: Summit Ridge Pinnacles
key_viewpoints.description: The shattered sandstone pinnacles on the summit provide otherworldly foreground interest against Suilven and Cul Mor.

torridon-an-teallach.photography_notes: An Teallach's Corrag Bhuidhe pinnacles form one of the most dramatic ridge features in Scotland. The traverse is spectacular.

key_viewpoints.name: Corrag Bhuidhe from Bidein a' Ghlas Thuill
key_viewpoints.description: Looking south along the pinnacle ridge from the northern top provides a dramatic profile of the Corrag Bhuidhe Buttresses.

torridon-slioch.photography_notes: Slioch is one of Scotland's most beautiful mountains when viewed across Loch Maree, a scene beloved by landscape photographers.

key_viewpoints.name: Loch Maree Viewpoint (A832)
key_viewpoints.description: Multiple pull-offs along the A832 offer classic views of Slioch rising above Loch Maree. Pine islands add foreground interest.

loch-lomond-ben-lomond.photography_notes: Ben Lomond provides sweeping views over Loch Lomond. The most southerly Munro is easily accessible for sunrise and sunset sessions.

key_viewpoints.name: Summit Viewpoint
key_viewpoints.description: The summit offers panoramic views south over the length of Loch Lomond. Clear days reveal the Arrochar Alps and beyond.
